name: Lean-donor FMT increased insulin sensitivity in metabolic syndrome
summary: Lean-donor intestinal microbiota transfer is the main human biomarker proof-of-concept for ecosystem replacement, but it is metabolic-disease rather than aging evidence.
statement: In male recipients with metabolic syndrome, lean-donor intestinal microbiota infusion increased insulin sensitivity at six weeks and increased levels of butyrate-producing intestinal microbiota compared with baseline.
time_horizon: Six weeks after allogenic or autologous microbiota infusion
quantitative_note: The abstract reports median glucose disappearance changed from 26.2 to 45.3 micromol/kg/min after lean-donor infusion, with P < .05.
caveats: The trial was small and disease-metabolic, not an older-adult aging or frailty trial.
The endpoint was insulin sensitivity and microbiota composition, not durable function, disease prevention, disability, or mortality.
The route and donor-selection context may differ from oral FMT capsule programs for aging.
